title: Fragaria aurea
intro: Fragaria aurea, also known as the golden strawberry or yellow strawberry, is a perennial plant species within the rose family (Rosaceae) that produces edible yellow fruits. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Fragaria aurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ellagic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fragaria aurea is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Fragaria aurea is a species of strawberry native to Mexico and Central America. It is classified within the family Rosaceae, which includes various other types of fruits and flowers. The genus Fragaria is a group of small, fruit-producing plants often referred to as strawberries. Fragaria aurea belongs to this genus and is further classified as a type of strawberry. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and shared traits with other species within the Fragaria genus.
